title: Right of Option Strategy
description: What are the conditions and implications of exercising the right of option to optimize unemployment benefits after returning to work?
narrative: The user is a jobseeker who has previously received unemployment benefits and has recently returned to work, either voluntarily or involuntarily. They are now re-registering with Pôle Emploi and are considering whether to exercise the 'right of option' to base their new unemployment allowance on their most recent, higher-paid employment rather than continuing to draw from their older, lower entitlements. The user needs to understand the eligibility criteria—such as having worked at least 910 hours (130 days) since the opening of their initial rights, receiving an allowance of no more than €20 per day, or qualifying for a 30% increase in their daily allowance—along with the consequences: permanently forfeiting their remaining old rights. The user must weigh the trade-off between a higher daily allowance over a shorter period versus continuing to receive lower payments for a longer duration. They also seek clarity on how the right of option interacts with their specific employment history, including any apprenticeship or professionalization contracts, and whether their current situation (e.g., part-time work, contract type, or timing of re-enrollment) affects their eligibility. The goal is to make an informed, strategic decision that maximizes financial support during their current unemployment period while aligning with their long-term career plans, such as starting a business or transitioning to a new job sector.

title: Car Puzzles and Games
description: Find online car-themed puzzles and games that combine vehicle imagery with interactive challenges suitable for children and casual players.
narrative: The user is seeking engaging, age-appropriate online activities that feature cars as a central theme, specifically within puzzle and game formats. Relevant documents include a free online puzzle site with car-themed puzzles (e.g., '180D PONTON 1957', 'The Little Queen', 'Vintage Taxi') and a collection of free online car games that include racing, driving, and stunt-based gameplay. The user likely wants content that is both entertaining and educational, suitable for children, with no need for downloads and accessible via browser. Relevance is determined by the presence of car-related imagery, vehicle types (e.g., vintage cars, sports cars, trucks), and interactive formats such as puzzles or games. Non-relevant documents, such as those about Meccano construction sets or unrelated games like chess or strategy titles, are excluded because they do not center on car-themed interactive puzzles or games.

title: Mobility Aid Eligibility
description: What are the conditions and criteria for receiving mobility assistance from Pôle emploi when attending a job interview or starting a new job far from home?
narrative: A job seeker in France is preparing for a job interview located more than 60 km from their residence and is unsure whether they qualify for financial support to cover travel, accommodation, and meal expenses. They are not currently receiving unemployment benefits and are seeking clarity on eligibility, application procedures, and the types of assistance available—such as direct reimbursement, SNCF travel vouchers, or accommodation through the SAM network. The user’s intent is to understand all available mobility aids, their specific conditions (e.g., distance, contract type, timing of application), and how to access them through Pôle emploi. Relevance is determined by documents that detail eligibility criteria, application timelines, covered expenses, and specific assistance options like travel vouchers or host family stays, excluding general overviews of Pôle emploi or unrelated financial aid such as car purchase loans or training stipends.

title: SFR Client Area Access
description: How can a user access their SFR Client Area to view bills, manage accounts, and track consumption using their login credentials?
narrative: The user is a current SFR customer who needs to access their client space to check invoices, monitor mobile and fixed-line usage, and manage account settings. They may have forgotten their login details or are unsure about the correct procedure to log in. Relevance is determined by documents that provide clear, step-by-step instructions for logging into the SFR Client Area, including how to recover passwords, verify identity, and use the My Account app or LICO platform. Documents discussing unrelated services such as MMS access, PUK code recovery, or phishing warnings are not relevant, as they do not address the core need of accessing the client area for account management and billing.

title: Oil Lamp Accessories
description: What are the best wicks and replacement parts for vintage and outdoor oil lamps?
narrative: The user is seeking information on high-quality, durable wicks and replacement components—such as glass chimneys, spouts, and fuel reservoirs—for both vintage and outdoor oil lamps. They are interested in materials like 100% natural cotton, treated fibers, or fiberglass that ensure efficient, clean burning and longevity. The user values authenticity, ease of replacement, and compatibility with various lamp types (e.g., storm lamps, retro designs, or artisanal models). Relevance is determined by documents that specifically discuss wick materials, dimensions, compatibility with lamp types, and accessories such as glass replacements, funnels, and adjustable wick mechanisms. Documents discussing fuel types without addressing hardware components are considered less relevant, as are general usage tips or fuel recommendations without accessory details.

title: Car Rental in Madeira
description: What are the best options and considerations for renting a car in Madeira to ensure a safe, cost-effective, and flexible travel experience across the island?
narrative: The user is planning a trip to Madeira and seeks comprehensive information about renting a car to explore the island efficiently. They want to understand the most cost-effective rental providers, the importance of booking in advance, and the key factors influencing rental prices—such as seasonality, vehicle type, and insurance options. The user is particularly concerned about navigating Madeira’s steep, winding roads and wants to ensure their rental includes full coverage (e.g., no deductible) and is suitable for mountainous terrain. They also want to know about pickup and drop-off flexibility, especially whether free delivery is available to hotels or remote locations. The user values transparency in pricing, avoids hidden fees, and prefers services with strong customer support and reliable online booking. Relevant documents provide detailed comparisons of rental platforms, pricing trends by month, vehicle recommendations (e.g., diesel for climbs), insurance inclusions, and delivery policies. Irrelevant documents focus on hiking trails and general travel tips, which do not address the core need of car rental logistics and decision-making.

title: Sprouted Potatoes Safety
description: What are the health risks of eating sprouted potatoes, and how should they be prepared to be safe for consumption?
narrative: The user is concerned about the safety of consuming sprouted potatoes, having encountered them during storage and heard conflicting advice about their toxicity. They seek a nuanced understanding of whether sprouted potatoes are dangerous, the role of solanine in causing harm, and practical steps to mitigate risk—such as removing sprouts and green parts—while also wanting to know if small amounts are safe. Relevance is determined by documents that discuss solanine toxicity, safe preparation methods, and the conditions under which sprouting occurs, including storage practices. Documents focused solely on planting, growing, or unrelated recipes (e.g., potato soup or baked potatoes) are not relevant.

title: Return-to-Work Bonus Eligibility
description: What are the conditions and eligibility criteria for receiving the return-to-work bonus after one month of employment, particularly for individuals transitioning from RSA or SSA to a CDI?
narrative: The user is a person who has recently secured a full-time CDI after a period of unemployment and receiving RSA. They are seeking clarification on whether they qualify for the return-to-work bonus after only one month of employment, despite the common requirement of four months. Their intent is to confirm their legal right to claim this bonus, especially given conflicting information from local agencies and online sources. Relevance is determined by documents that explicitly address the eligibility conditions, duration of employment, required benefits (such as RSA or SSA), and the role of Pôle Emploi or CAF in processing the claim. Documents discussing the discontinuation of the bonus after 2010 or its replacement by APRE are relevant only if they clarify the transitional rules or exceptions for early claims. Documents about unrelated benefits like the Christmas bonus or employment tax credits are not relevant.
